content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

September 19, 1990

Gary Morrison
Deputy General Counsel
University of California
300 Lakeside Drive
Oakland, CA  94612

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-90-538

Dear Mr. Morrison:

This is in response to your letter of August 16, 1990,
seeking advice regarding the responsibilities and duties under the
conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act ("the
Act")¹ of Meredith J. Khachigian, a member of the Board of Regents
of the University of California.

QUESTIONS

In light of the financial interests of Regent Khachigian,
described below:

(1) May Regent Khachigian participate in the deliberations
and votes of the site selection task force in connection with
recommendations it makes to the President of the University who
will, in turn, make a recommendation to the Board?

(2) May Regent Khachigian participate in decisions by the
Board to narrow the list of sites and to choose the final site?

CONCLUSIONS

(1) Regent Khachigian may participate in the deliberations
and votes of the site selection task force in connection with
recommendations it makes to the President of the University who

_____________
¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All section references
are to the Government Code.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

Our File No. A-90-538
Page 2


will, in turn, make a recommendation to the Board, because the
owner of the affected property has not been a source of income to
the Regent during the 12 months preceding such participation.

(2) Regent Khachigian may participate in decisions by the
Board to narrow the list of sites and to choose the final site
because the owner of the affected property has not been a source
of income to the Regent during the 12 months preceding such
participation.

FACTS

1.  Meredith J. Khachigian is a member of the Board of Regents of
the University of California (the "Board").

2.  In November 1988, the Board authorized University of
California President, David P. Gardner, to begin the process
of planning for possible new campuses of the University.

3.  In March 1989, President Gardner appointed a site selection
task force (the "task force") made up of Regent Khachigian,
two other members of the Board, and six members of the
University faculty and staff. The task force is charged with
advising the President on the development and application of
criteria and methodology for choosing new campus sites. The
President considers the recommendations of the task force in
making his recommendations to the Board, which makes the
final decisions.

4.  In order to avoid any appearance of conflict of interest in
the site selection process, The Regents adopted special
financial disclosure requirements that before any action is
taken by the Board to narrow the list of potential sites,
members of the Board disclose any economic or personal
interest they or members of their families may have related
to identified potential sites. The first such disclosure was
due in connection with the July 19, 1990, meeting of the
Board to consider reducing to eight the sites under
consideration in the central region of the state. In early
July, in the process of making this disclosure, Regent
Khachigian was informed that a portion of one of the eight
sites was owned by Mr. Richard Gunner. Regent Khachigian's
husband, Kenneth, an attorney, had once performed legal
services as described below for Mr. Gunner on behalf of the
Gunner Ranch (hereafter "Gunner").

5.  In January 1989, prior to Regent Khachigian's appointment to
the task force, and in connection with matters unrelated to
the University of California, Gunner contacted Mr. Khachigian
regarding advice on infrastructure development in the
vicinity of property he owned in Madera County--one parcel of
which is now among the eight sites referenced above.

Our File No. A-90-538
Page 3


6.     On April 20, 1989, Mr. Khachigian received a retainer fee of
more than $500 from Gunner.  Thereafter, Mr. Khachigian
performed legal services for Gunner related to their
discussion in January.  These legal services were
discontinued as of July 6, 1989, and no professional fees
were received by Mr. Khachigian subsequent to April 20.
However, Mr. Khachigian did receive reimbursement for direct
out-of-pocket expenses in the sum of $523.88 on June 7, 1989,
and in the sum of $263.92 on August 7, 1989.  The August 7
payment was the final reimbursement of Mr. Khachigian by
Gunner.  Regent Khachigian has a community property interest
of less than $250 in the August 7 payment and of more than
$250 in the June 7 payment as well as the April 20 fee.  No
income of any kind has been received by Mr. Khachigian from
Gunner during the last 12 months.  Neither Mr. Khachigian nor
Regent Khachigian has engaged in any other contracts or
business transactions with Gunner.

7.     At all times during the task force's deliberations and until
July 11, 1990, the sites being considered by the task force
were presented to its members by code designations.  So, for
example, the site which includes the Gunner property was
never referred to by any of its owners' names, but as MD 1.
At the June 8, 1990, task force meeting--at which some 20
sites were brought in by the University staff with
recommendations to reduce the selection to seven--the final
sites were still referenced by code designations.  On June 8,
the task force received and accepted the recommendations of
its staff.

8.     On June 21, 1990, President Gardner wrote to all the members
of the Board, enclosing a list of counties in which the sites
were located in order that further information could be
provided to members who believed these counties might
potentially require further disclosure.  Regent and Mr.
Khachigian were out of the state on vacation from July 1 to
July 8, 1990.  Upon returning, the two of them reviewed
President Gardner's communication, and on July 11 replied by
facsimile transmission that three counties would require
further information--Tulare, Fresno and Madera.  The
Khachigians have numerous relatives in Tulare and Fresno
Counties, and Mr. Khachigian and previous (though no active)
clients in all three counties.

9.     On July 11, 1990, Regent Khachigian made specific inquiries
to University personnel about the sites and was informed that
Mr. Gunner owned property in the site previously referred to
as MD 1.  That was the first time Regent Khachigian became
aware that a site under consideration by the task force and
the Board included real property owned by her husband's
former client.

10.    Due to a previous commitment, Regent Khachigian did not
attend the July 19, 1990, meeting of the Board at which the

Our File No. A-90-538
Page 4


Board voted to further consider the eight sites referred to
it, including the parcel owned or controlled by Gunner.
Thus, from the time Regent Khachigian learned of the
ownership by Gunner, she has not participated in any decision
relating to new site selection. On July 18, 1990, based on
the foregoing facts, your office rendered an opinion to
Regent Khachigian that she did not have a disqualifying
financial interest and could participate fully in site
selection issues.

11. Absent any disqualifying financial interest, Regent
Khachigian would anticipate participating in the following
activities of the task force and the Board:

a. In late September or early October the members of the
task force plan to personally visit each of the eight
sites currently under consideration. You anticipate
that the sites will be the subject of informal
discussion among members of the task force, but no
action will be taken or recommendation made at that
time.

b. Coincident with the site visits described above, the
task force will hold a series of public forums in the
areas of the potential sites. The task force plans to
listen to persons in affected communities; no action
will be taken or recommendation made by the task force
at that time.

c. On November 9, 1990, the site selection task force will
have a meeting to consider available information about
the eight sites under consideration. You anticipate
that each of the eight sites will then be subject to a
vote by Regent Khachigian and the members of the task
force as to whether it should be recommended to
President Gardner. The task force will then forward the
results of its vote to President Gardner to use in
connection with his recommendation to the Board of
Regents at the November meeting, when the Board plans to
reduce the eight sites to two or three. In voting on
whether to forward the sites for further consideration
by President Gardner, the task force will be informed by
a number of reports from outside consultants with
respect to the criteria applicable to narrowing the
sites at this stage. The professional staff of
President Gardner will also offer its recommendations to
assist the site selection task force in its
considerations.

d. At its November 1990 meeting the Board, including Regent
Khachigian, is scheduled to vote to narrow the eight
sites currently under consideration to two or three for
more intensive further consideration.

Our File No. A-90-538
Page 5


e.    The current schedule calls for the Board, including
Regent Khachigian, to vote to choose a site for a new
campus at its November 1991 meeting after considering
environmental impact report(s) and additional detailed
data on the sites remaining under consideration.

ANALYSIS

Section 87103 provides:

"An official has a financial interest in a decision
within the meaning of Section 87100 if it is reasonably
foreseeable that the decision will have a material
financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the
public generally, on the official or a member of his or
her immediate family or on:

(a)   Any business entity in which the public
official has a direct or indirect investment worth one
thousand dollars ($1,000) or more.

(b)   Any real property in which the public official
has a direct or indirect interest worth one thousand
dollars ($1,000) or more.

(c)   Any source of income, other than gifts and
other than loans by a commercial lending institution in
the regular course of business on terms available to the
public without regard to official status, aggregating
two hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value
provided to, received by or promised to the public
official within 12 months prior to the time when the
decision is made.

(d)   Any business entity in which the public
official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of management.

(e)   Any donor of, or any intermediary or agent for
a donor of, a gift or gifts aggregating two hundred
fifty dollars ($250) or more in value provided to,
received by, or promised to the public official within
12 months prior to the time when the decision is made.

"For purposes of this section, indirect investment
or interest means any investment or interest owned by
the spouse or dependent child of a public official, by
an agent on behalf of a public official, or by a
business entity or trust in which the official, the
official's agents, spouse, and dependent children own
directly, indirectly, or beneficially a 10-percent
interest or greater."

Our File No. A-90-538
Page 6


     Only subsection (c) of section 87103 would seem to be
applicable to the facts stated above. However, Regent Khachigian
has not received income, either directly or through her spouse,
from Mr. Gunnar during the 12 month period preceding future
Board activities in which the Regent would be participating.
Thus, Mr. Gunnar would not be a disqualifying "source of income"
for those activities.

     Since no disqualifying economic interest appears from the
facts stated in your letter as to future activities of the Board
of Regents, Regent Khachigian need not disqualify herself from the
planned participation.

     The foregoing advice is limited to the facts stated in your
letter as to future conduct of the Regent. As you stated in our
recent telephone conversation, you did not intend to seek our
advice as to past conduct. Furthermore, we do not advise with
respect to past conduct. (Regulation 18329(c).)

     I trust this letter has provided you with the guidance you
requested. For your convenience, a copy of your incoming letter
is enclosed.

     If you have any further questions regarding this matter
please contact me at (916) 322-5091.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Scott Hallabrin
                                        Acting General Counsel

                                        By: Joseph Garcia
                                            Senior Commission Counsel,
                                            Legal Division
